content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
May 4, 1989 
David L. Gould 
609 South Grand Avenue, suite 500 
Los Angeles, CA 
90017 
Dear Mr. Gould: 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-89-202 
This letter confirms the telephone advice I provided to you 
on April 7, 1989, concerning the transfer of Assemblywoman Roybal 
Allard's "restricted funds" from her new campaign bank account for 
future candidacy to her original campaign account for moneys 
collected prior to January 1, 1989. 
This letter also confirms 
that I advised you that the Assemblywoman must create a new 
committee for the funds received after January 1, 1989. 
(Regulation 18521, copy enclosed.) 
In our telephone conversation, I cautioned that "restricted 
funds" cannot be used to support or oppose a candidacy for 
elective office. 
(Section 85306.) 
Moreover, in the event that 
Regulations 18536(b) (2) and 18536.1 are upheld by an appellate 
court in the future, the campaign funds that Assemblywoman Roybal-
Allard has transferred to the original campaign bank account 
cannot be used to support or oppose a candidacy for elective 
office. 
If you have any questions about this letter, please contact 
me at (916) 322-5901. 
Sincerely, 
/

Dear Ms. Donovan: 
Th1s letter Is to cegueat written confirmation of telephone Advice 
provided to me by you regarding the campaign provisions of the 
Political Reform Act as they relate to the campaign committees of 
Aseemblywoman Lucille Roybal-Allard. 
The campaign funds of the Assemblywoman were separated according to 
the regulations In effect at the end of 1988. 
The -good- or 
unrestricted funds were put Into a new bank account under a new 
account name. 
Though a 501 and 502 were filed no new committee was 
formed and a 410 was not filed. The Assemblywoman would now like to 
transfer the monies that were separated back to the original 
restrlcted account to be u~ed for officeholder expenses. 
She will 
also fIle a 410 for a new committee and will raise money for that 
comm1ttee. 
P.02 
The advice you gave me was that It would be permissible to transfer 
the funds that were separated back to the orlglnal Hrestrlcted" 
dccount and that we would need to file a 410 as to the new committee. 
Please send a confirmation letter to me at the address listed above, 
If you have any questions about this letter, please call me at (213) 
489-4792. 
Sincerely, 
David L.Gould 


01 


[12 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
April 12, 1989 
David L. Gould 
609 South Grand Avenue, suite 500 
Los Angeles, CA 
90017 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-202 
Dear Mr. Gould: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act 
was received on April 7, 1989 by the Fair political Practices 
Commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact Kathryn Donovan an attorney in the Legal Division, 
directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response 
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to 
information needed. 
If your request is for informal assistance, 
we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission 
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
Very truly yours, 
~ ~

Diane M. Griffiths

sections.question: The campaign funds of the Assemblywoman were separated according to the regulations in effect at the end of 1988. The 'good' or unrestricted funds were put into a new bank account under a new account name. Though a 501 and 502 were filed no new committee was formed and a 410 was not filed. The Assemblywoman would now like to transfer the monies that were separated back to the original restricted account to be used for officeholder expenses. She will also file a 410 for a new committee and will raise money for that committee.
sections.conclusion: The transfer of funds from the new campaign bank account for future candidacy to the original campaign account for moneys collected prior to January 1, 1989 is permissible. A new committee must be created for funds received after January 1, 1989. 'Restricted funds' cannot be used to support or oppose a candidacy for elective office, and campaign funds transferred to the original account cannot be used to support or oppose a candidacy if Regulations 18536(b)(2) and 18536.1 are upheld by appellate court.
sections.question_synthetic: May Assemblywoman Roybal-Allard transfer separated unrestricted campaign funds back to her original restricted account, and what procedures must be followed regarding new committee formation and fund handling?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advised that the fund transfer is permissible and a new committee must be created for post-January 1, 1989 funds, but restricted funds cannot be used to support or oppose candidacy for elective office.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes incoming request (April 11, 1989), acknowledgment letter (April 12, 1989), and FPPC response (May 4, 1989). Extracted information from the primary advice letter dated May 4, 1989. No formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION section headers, but substantive content clearly demarcates the question and conclusion.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The campaign funds of the Assemblywoman were separated according to the regulations in effect at the end of 1988. The 'good' or unrestricted funds were put into a new bank account under a new account name. Though a 501 and 502 were filed no new committee was formed and a 410 was not filed. The Assemblywoman would now like to transfer the monies that were separated back to the original restricted account to be used for officeholder expenses. She will also file a 410 for a new committee and will raise money for that committee.

CONCLUSION: The transfer of funds from the new campaign bank account for future candidacy to the original campaign account for moneys collected prior to January 1, 1989 is permissible. A new committee must be created for funds received after January 1, 1989. 'Restricted funds' cannot be used to support or oppose a candidacy for elective office, and campaign funds transferred to the original account cannot be used to support or oppose a candidacy if Regulations 18536(b)(2) and 18536.1 are upheld by appellate court.

embedding.summary: FPPC General Counsel Kathryn Donovan provided written confirmation of telephone advice to David L. Gould regarding Assemblywoman Lucille Roybal-Allard's campaign fund management, including permitting transfer of separated funds back to the original restricted account and requiring formation of a new committee for future funds.
